note: hero_walk 2차 리젝 대응: 6프레임 워크사이클, 프레임별 생체역학(다리 시저링, 상하 바디밥, 팔 카운터스윙) 명시. identity=hero_idle_strip. 수용 기준=육안으로 다리 교차 + 상하 밥 확인.

jobs.prompt: Pixel art 6-frame walk-cycle sprite strip in the classic 16-bit action-RPG side-scroller tradition: exactly 6 frames arranged in a single horizontal row (1x6 grid), 6 equal-width columns, one full-body character per column, generous empty magenta space between and around every column so the columns never touch, no drop shadows on the background. The SAME character appears in all 6 frames at exactly the same size and identity: the cute brave elementary school student adventurer boy shown in the small reference sprite, messy brown hair, small flowing red cape, white short-sleeve shirt, blue trousers and red shoes, viewed from a 3/4 top-down angle facing slightly toward the camera while walking to the left. This is a real biomechanical walk cycle where the legs clearly SCISSOR (alternate) and the torso BOBS up and down. Per frame: FRAME 1 CONTACT-LEFT - left leg planted forward and straight with the foot flat on the ground bearing weight, right leg reaching back with its heel lifted off the ground and knee bent, RIGHT arm swung forward and LEFT arm swung back with elbows slightly bent, torso at MEDIUM height, cape trailing back. FRAME 2 RECOIL - both feet near the ground with the front knee bent absorbing weight, body dropped to its LOWEST point, arms passing near vertical. FRAME 3 PASSING-LEFT - left support leg now straight and vertical carrying all the weight, right leg lifted and swinging forward underneath the body with knee high and foot off the ground, body at its HIGHEST point, arms roughly at the sides mid-swing, hair bouncing up. FRAME 4 CONTACT-RIGHT - mirror of frame 1: right leg planted forward and straight foot flat bearing weight, left leg reaching back with heel lifted and knee bent, LEFT arm swung forward and RIGHT arm swung back, torso at MEDIUM height, cape trailing. FRAME 5 RECOIL - both feet near the ground, front knee bent, body at its LOWEST point again, arms near vertical. FRAME 6 PASSING-RIGHT - right support leg straight and vertical, left leg lifted and swinging forward under the body knee high foot off the ground, body at its HIGHEST point, hair bouncing. Across the six frames the two legs must be visibly in DIFFERENT positions in every frame, never both feet flat side by side in a neutral stance, the front foot and back foot separated by a clear stride gap on the contact frames, and the head height must visibly rise on the passing frames and dip on the recoil frames. All frames share the same ground line under the planted foot.
